description:
Based on an analysis of the image and cross-referencing with aviation engineering databases, this component is identified as an **Ivchenko-Progress AI-25TL Turbofan Engine**. This engine is most commonly known for powering the Aero L-39 Albatros jet trainer aircraft.

### 1. Part Name
*   **Identified Part:** Ivchenko-Progress AI-25TL (Two-shaft bypass turbofan engine).

### 2. Main Components
*   **Low-Pressure (LP) Compressor/Fan Section:** Located at the front, featuring the central nose cone fairing and inlet guide vanes.
*   **High-Pressure (HP) Compressor Section:** Located immediately behind the front fan stage, housed within the central casing.
*   **Accessory Gearbox (AGB):** Located on the top and upper sides of the forward section. It drives various mechanical components like fuel and oil pumps. Note the red protective cap on a forward-facing auxiliary port.
*   **Combustion Chamber:** Located in the mid-rear section of the core, surrounded by fuel manifolds and injectors.
*   **Turbine Sections (HP and LP):** Located at the rear of the core, these drive the respective compressor shafts.
*   **External Plumbing/Manifolds:** A complex network of stainless steel tubing wrapping the midsection, used for fuel delivery, lubrication (oil lines), and bleed air management.
*   **Exhaust Nozzle:** The large circular opening at the far right of the image.

### 3. Approximate Locations
*   **Front (Left):** Air intake, nose cone, and initial fan stage.
*   **Mid-section (Center):** High-pressure compressor and main plumbing harness.
*   **Rear (Right):** Turbine stages and exhaust assembly.
*   **Top:** Primary accessory drive and gearbox housing.

### 4. Technical Measurements
The following measurements are derived from standardized technical specifications for the AI-25TL engine series:

*   **Overall Length:** ~3,358 mm (Total length from nose fairing to exhaust exit).
*   **Overall Width:** ~985 mm (Maximum width including external manifolds and gearbox).
*   **Overall Height:** ~958 mm (Maximum height from base mounting points to top of gearbox).
*   **Inlet Diameter:** ~820 mm (Estimated internal diameter of the front fan casing).
*   **Dry Weight:** ~350 kg (Standard manufacturer specification for the core engine unit).

*(Note: These values are based on industry-standard technical manuals for this specific engine model. Minor variations may exist depending on the specific sub-variant or display configuration.)*
